rationale: EV-001 shows reg.exe executed at High integrity level by host-01\user-01 from C:\Users\IT01-user-01\Downloads\ — an atypical working directory for administrative registry changes — with the explicit command to set HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\EventLog\Start to 4 (disabled), confirmed written by A0. Disabling the Windows Event Log service is a well-known defense-evasion technique used to suppress audit trails. The parent process is cmd.exe with no further command-line context visible, and the Downloads directory origin raises concern about a dropped script or tool. However, no confirmed malicious payload, lateral movement, or persistence mechanism is evidenced in this package, and a legitimate administrator could theoretically perform this action, so the verdict stops short of malicious pending further investigation of the parent cmd.exe session and the Downloads directory contents.
